source_pr.title: Optimizer : Add OR pruning to predicate simplification | tidb-test=pr/2397
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #56005

### Problem Summary:
For this predicate it.a = "a" AND (it.a > "a" OR it.a = "a" AND it.pk > 1), the condition  it.a > "a" in the OR can be pruned since 
- This expression can be rewritten as (it.a = "a" AND it.a > "a") OR (it.a = "a" AND it.a = "a" AND it.pk > 1) 
- (it.a = "a" AND it.a > "a") is false
- false OR (it.a = "a" AND it.a = "a" AND it.pk > 1)  is (it.a = "a" AND it.a = "a" AND it.pk > 1) . 
This logic is already implemented in the optimizer but it is limited when the collation/type of column and constant are not the same. In this example, it.a is binary and "a" is string. The restriction is too strict since the string value is treated like binary and not casting is applied.

### What changed and how does it work?
We can safely apply the pruning logic in this case since no casting is done and we can easily drive "a" > "a" which is false.
The change is applied to the new predicate simplification code and not the legacy code which need to be refactored in another PR.  The main idea for P & (P1 OR P2, ... OR Pn), we prune Pi when P &Pi is false. If all Pi are pruned then the whole predicate is false.

Documentation

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
